questions.text: In general, does site leadership understand the legal OH&S and DP requirements & how these obligations are fulfilled?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site leadership can demonstrate general knowledge of their legal obligations in OH&S and DP management and how to fulfil these.

questions.text: Does Site Leadership consider safety a priority?
questions.judgement_criteria: Interview statements from workers indicate that safety is considered a priority by leadership. "

questions.text: Is Site Leadership involved in the OH&S management when a serious incident occurs?
questions.judgement_criteria: Communication records and statements from workers indicate that leadership is involved in OH&S management when serious incidents(lost-time-injury, fire accident, etc.) occur.

questions.text: Does line management actively provide feedback to teammates and promote safety while touring their department/area?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site tour(s) indicate that line-management conducts floor observations to ensure adherence to safety rules, such as procedures, standards, etc.

questions.text: Do teammates know what their responsibilities are when it comes to Safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: Interview statements from teammates and line-managers indicate that they accept own responsibilities when it comes to safety management and the importance of their contributions.

questions.text: Do teammates report hazards (e.g., near misses, hidden dangers) regularly? (:
questions.judgement_criteria: Hazard reporting records indicate that teammates are actively involved in reporting and addressing hazards and risks in their daily jobs

questions.text: Are good safety behaviors of teammates rewarded and recognized by managements??
questions.judgement_criteria: Good safety behaviors of teammates are rewarded and recognized by managements.

questions.text: Is there an external/cross-plant safety meeting? If so, does safety manager share H&S issues or best practices?
questions.judgement_criteria: Management contributes to cross-plant and external H&S meetings during which H&S challenges and insights are shared

questions.text: Is there a long term OH&S strategy for the plant?
questions.judgement_criteria: Site leadership has incorporated the OH&S program in their overall strategy and can explain how the conflicting objectives are prevented in that strategy

questions.text: Do teammates feel comfortable stopping work if they feel there is a risk of injury?
questions.judgement_criteria: All interviewed workers confirm that safety is essential and that they feel comfortable stopping work whenever health or safety might be compromised

questions.text: Have applicable site-specific OH&S regulations been identified, and site-specific safety procedures been implemented that they are compliant with local regulation and SBU standards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Applicable site-specific OH&S regulations have been identified and current safety procedures and processes have been evaluated to ensure they are compliant with local regulation. SBU requirements.
Applicable site-specific OH&S regulations have been identified and current safety procedures and processes have been evaluated to ensure they are compliant with local regulation. SBU requirements.

questions.text: Are safety results (KPIs) being monitored by leadership?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety performance (Safety KPIs) based on OH&S policy is monitored and tracked by site leadership. KPI
Safety performance (Safety KPIs) based on OH&S policy is monitored and tracked by site leadership. KPI

questions.text: Are the root causes of incidents categorized and then tracked?
questions.judgement_criteria: Incident investigation records identify the root causes of incidents, categorizes them, and tracks the number of incidents by each category. (Human error, system error, Natural causes) *()

questions.text: Are there repercussions/consequences for not following the Safety Rules and procedures? /
questions.judgement_criteria: Leadership often emphasizes the importance of the Safety Rules and breaking the rules results in repercussions/consequences.

questions.text: Does Leadership use "Leading indicators" in their safety result tracking? KPI
questions.judgement_criteria: Leadership keeps track of leading indicators to determine the results of investments made to improve health and safety at the facility
Leadership keeps track of leading indicators to determine the results of investments made to improve health and safety at the facility

questions.text: Are pre and post assessments used to determine the effectiveness of OH&S initiatives?
questions.judgement_criteria: Managers and/or the safety department are conduct pre- and post- assessments to determine the effectiveness of OH&S initiatives /()

questions.text: Does leadership motivate teams to find and implement new best practices relevant to safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: Leadership motivate teams to find the best safety practices and provide the resources to implement them.

questions.text: Are the OH&S management processes required by regulations executed on time?
questions.judgement_criteria: Review of records indicates that legally required OH&S management processes are implemented in a consistent manner.

questions.text: Are safety initiatives being implemented to improve safety or improve operational results?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety Initiatives are being implemented to improve safety results, not operational performance results.

questions.text: Does leadership know how important teammate involvement in safety is, and involve teammates in safety activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Leadership understand the importance of teammate involvement in safety management and can demonstrate clear actions regularly incorporated to help the site realize the positive results of those actions.

questions.text: Is there a H&S meeting with representation from teammates, leadership and H&S, and leadership give directions to solve issues?
questions.judgement_criteria: The H&S team, teammates and leadership meet at planned intervals to provide inputs to improve safety on site.

questions.text: Are there ongoing safety projects addressing safety issues?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is a clear OH&S program consisting of multiple improvement/enhancement projects and allocated budgets to address defined OH&S priorities

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on safety activity from GBS/SBU or external companies, and implement them in the plant? GBS/SBU
questions.judgement_criteria: The safety team can demonstrate how industry(tire industry, rubber industry, manufacturing industry, etc.) *best-practice and scientifically proven best-practices are incorporated in their H&S program ( *

questions.text: Are general H&S risks explained during new-hire training to new teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates are made aware of general H&S risks during new hire training.

questions.text: Is management aware of their personal impact on safety and do they leverage this to improve safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: Management is aware of their responsibility in keeping teammates safe and enforcing safety rules and procedures.

questions.text: Do teammates know how to report incidents (injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss/hidden danger)?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates know how to report when incident(injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss, hidden danger, fire) occurs, and incident reporting records and interview statements indicate that teammates report incidents. ()

questions.text: Do teammates know the department/job safety rules applicable to them? /
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates demonstrate a clear understanding of the H&S rules involved in their work and the relevance of these rules

questions.text: Do you understand the greatest potential risks, and how they are controlled?
questions.judgement_criteria: Interview statements indicate that teammates take trainings and understand the greatest potential H&S risks involved in their work and the controls applied to reduce these risks.

questions.text: Have teammates proposed improvements of H&S risks and risk mitigation measures at the creations or revisions of work standards, training materials, etc.
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates propose improvements of H&S risks and risk mitigation measures at the creations or revisions of work standard, training material, etc.

questions.text: Are some teammates capable of performing full risk assessments?
questions.judgement_criteria: A sample of teammates participate in the risk assessment (RA) process, and they can demonstrate capability in performing a risk assessment on their work activities

questions.text: Do teammates know safety related information (H&S risk, recurrence prevention measures issued by GBS/SBU, etc.) shared among teams (department/areas) and plants? /GBS/SBU
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates are encouraged to talk with other teams and speak about H&S risks regularly to share best-practices and lessons-learned.

questions.text: Do you think that safety is considered more important than production?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates know that safety is considered more important than production.

questions.text: Are teammates involved in preventing recurring incidents (injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss/hidden danger)?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates are involved in the actions implemented to prevent the reoccurrence of an incident (injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss, hidden danger, fire) relevant to their daily job to ensure the appropriateness of such actions ()

questions.text: Are the Department/Job Safety rules being followed strictly? /
questions.judgement_criteria: Site tour(s) indicates a structural adherence to the Department/Job Safety Rules applied to the sampled working areas.

questions.text: Have teammates proposed improvements for @reventation of incidents (injure/near-miss/hidden danger) and management has given feedback to them?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates proposed improvements to prevent incidents (injure, occupational disease/illness, near-miss/hidden danger) and received feedback from managements.

questions.text: Are teammates involved and consulted in safety action plans?
questions.judgement_criteria: Review of action plans show that teammates have been involved in the design, implementation and evaluation of safety action plans.

questions.text: Is unsafe behavior completely unacceptable on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: Interview statements and site tour(s) indicate that unsafe behavior is perceived as unacceptable. It is that anyone acting unsafe will be corrected by their peers.

questions.text: Do managers explain safety requirements based on site-specific regulation to teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Managers explain safety requirements based on regulation to teammate.

questions.text: Are teammates informed of recent safety incidents (injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss/hidden danger) in their areas and know what actions have been taken to address these?
questions.judgement_criteria: Statements from teammates indicate that they are informed of recent safety incidents relevant to them and the follow-up/actions taken to address them.

questions.text: Are teammates regularly trained about the departmental/job safety rules applicable to them? /
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates are trained at planned intervals of the different H&S rules aimed at ensuring their safety.

questions.text: Have teammates taken training on residual risks based on the work standard
questions.judgement_criteria: Interview statements indicate that teammates take trainings and understand the greatest potential H&S risks involved in their work and the controls applied to reduce these risks.

questions.text: Are there opportunities to share H&S risks/measures of other departments at the safety meeting in your department? /()
questions.judgement_criteria: H&S risks/measures of other departments have been shared at the safety meeting in your department. /

questions.text: Do you have opportunities to get *best practices from internal/external sources, and do you implement them in the plant? *
questions.judgement_criteria: The site safety department communicates their best-practices in risk control with other plants to ensure cross-company learning and awareness.

questions.text: Is the legally required H&S training (including safety/disaster emergency drills) given to all teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Compliance evaluation and training records indicate that H&S training required by regulation (including safety/disaster emergency drills) has been provided to all teammates.

questions.text: Are incident investigations carried out by qualified people?
questions.judgement_criteria: Incident(injury, fire occupational disease/illness, near-miss, hidden danger)investigations are conducted by qualified people and the identified causes are included in the risk tracking as to-be-controlled risks (SE

questions.text: Are current Safety rule's effectiveness being evaluated by a team?
questions.judgement_criteria: The H&S team, teammates and their supervisors evaluate the design, implementation and effectiveness of current safety rules in order to optimize these

questions.text: Is there a skill matrix table for teammates that identifies the training requirements/needs for each job, including training dates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Specific safety trainings are repeated at a standard interval to ensure the competence of people working on site. There are mandatory training requirements for each activity.

questions.text: Do work/job specific trainings highlight safety risks and processes associated with that work/job?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety instructions and trainings have been integrated into regular job-related training which is provided to teammates before they start the associated work.

questions.text: Is risk assessment one of the first actions taken when planning new projects/work sessions? /1RA
questions.judgement_criteria: Risks are assessed at the earliest possible planning stages of every team in the organization to avoid risks where they might materialize.

questions.text: Do teammates communicate injuries/near-misses/defects to managers, and does management follow-up on them? //
questions.judgement_criteria: Incidents (injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss, hidden danger) defects are communicated to management and they are encouraged to follow-up.

questions.text: Is passive communication, such as notification boards and e-mails, being used to convey safety information?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates are made aware of the function of safety controls through passive communication channels such as notice boards and e-mails

questions.text: Does management know what safety initiatives are active on site and how they are performing, and impact on the site's lagging indicators?
questions.judgement_criteria: H and S activities are observed/reviewed by management for results and activities have the opportunity for continuous improvement about the lagging performance indication (e.g. # of injury, frequency rate, etc.) .

questions.text: Do teammates know who to speak to about H&S/DP and how the reporting structure works?
questions.judgement_criteria: Interview statements indicate that teammates know who to report to in order to fulfil their H&S/DP relevant responsibilities

questions.text: Is there a system on management of changes?
questions.judgement_criteria: Forepersons/supervisors understand the system and scope of management of changes(4M changes) 4M

questions.text: Do management and process owners communicate with H&S team about upcoming changes on site, along with any risks associated with the change?
questions.judgement_criteria: Prior to a change in process or equipment, are the risks associated with the change reviewed, evaluated and communicated with H&S team.

questions.text: Does management take action when a hazard/injury/near-miss/defects is reported, and communicate these action to the reporter? ///
questions.judgement_criteria: Risk records indicate that hazards/injury/near-miss/defects reported are used by management to take actions and the result of these actions are communicated with the reporter. ///

questions.text: Do you receive feedback from third party on effectiveness of safety management system
questions.judgement_criteria: For the improvement of safety management system of the plant, external party evaluate the system.

questions.text: Does management have defined roles and responsibilities for safety activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Roles and responsibilities of safety activities set out, and they are understood by those required to fulfil them

questions.text: Is there collaboration between H&S and management to address recurring incidents, with focus on causation?
questions.judgement_criteria: Management and the H&S team collaborate to prevent reoccurrence of accidents by addressing the causation of these accidents

questions.text: Do safety training sessions for newcomers reference relevant frequent incidents and risks?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety training for newcomers includes reference to *frequent incidents(injury, occupational disease/illness, near-miss, hidden danger)and severe risks to ensure teammates understand these and corresponding controls. *Frequent incident means that same incident is re-occurring

questions.text: Are all safety programs reviewed periodically? 1
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety procedures are periodically reviewed to identify opportunities for improvement. This is often done by the H&S team and the line-managers involved in the specific safety processes. ()()

questions.text: Are safety procedures and processes frequency reviewed and updated using findings from the review?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety procedures and processes are reviewed at regular intervals by those involved, and revisions are made based upon relevant findings.

questions.text: Do teammates and managers have a genuine concern for themselves and one another? When there are safety relates issues, is a CFT approach taken to resolving them? CFT
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates and managers are mindful of the need for safety, not only for themselves but also for others. Teammates and managers have a genuine concern for one another; a CFT approach is used to review and address safety issues. CFT

questions.text: Do line managers engage with external parties about safety?
questions.judgement_criteria: Line managers are actively engaging with *external parties to obtain knowledge on how they have controlled their risks and exchange knowledge on safety processes regularly. *External includes other company, SBU, other plants in BS * *SBU

questions.text: Are the regulations concerning safety training known at the site?
questions.judgement_criteria: All teammates receive training on the safety mission statement, *high-risk activities and the regulation for training are known. *High-risk activity: Activity which are risk category III&IV. E.g. Work at height, heavy object handling, mobile equipment, caught by machine, tire inflation, etc. *III&IV

questions.text: Are lists of approved chemicals, Material Use Forms and Safety Data Sheets kept according to local regulation? SDS
questions.judgement_criteria: Is there a list of all approved chemicals for the site, along with a corresponding Material Use Form (MUF) and Safety Data Sheet (SDS), and these records are kept according to local regulation.Required employees are aware of Chemical hazards. SDS

questions.text: Are you aware of laws and regulations regarding flammable materials?
questions.judgement_criteria: The flammable materials are listed and these list are frequently and properly maintained.

questions.text: When conducting hot work, do you review a fire watcher assignment?
questions.judgement_criteria: When conducting hot work, the required control measures are correctly recognized.

questions.text: Do you perform the periodic inspection of the fire extinguishing equipment in accordance with laws and regulations?
questions.judgement_criteria: The fire extinguishing equipment is properly managed and operated.

questions.text: Is Mixing MUST11 deployed to mixing department and maintenance department? MUST11
questions.judgement_criteria: Mixing MUST 11 is deployed and educated in sites. MUST11

questions.text: Is the LOTO standard reviewed after incidents, audits or when opportunity areas have been identified? LOTO
questions.judgement_criteria: The LOTO standard(site standard, ECP, work standard, etc.) is reviewed in response to incidents, audit findings, authority guidance and concerns raised by teammates LOTOECP* *

questions.text: Is the effectiveness of the Site Safety Rules reviewed after incidents, audits or opportunity areas have been identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: The effectiveness of Site Safety Rules is reviewed in response to incidents, audits and authority's guidance.

questions.text: Is the Work at height procedure and its trainings reviewed after a Work at height incident has occurred?
questions.judgement_criteria: Work at height training and controls are reviewed and improved when incidents involving Work at height have occurred.

questions.text: Is there any accidents on mobile equipment last one year? Is the Mobile Equipment procedure and its trainings reviewed after an incident involving mobile equipment has occurred?
questions.judgement_criteria: The implementation and design of mobile equipment safety controls are reviewed and improved after incidents occur.

questions.text: Are you trained how to use the fire extinguishing equipment?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates understand how to use the fire extinguisher.

questions.text: Do you standardize how to maintain the electrical wirings?
questions.judgement_criteria: Electric system inspection method are trained to person in charge. /

questions.text: Are trainings reviewed and adjusted when safety rules and controls that involve that specific subject matter change?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training curricula are reviewed when adjustments are made to safety rules and controls significant to that training.

questions.text: Are all risks in the risk register covered by administrative or technical controls? Besides, are the remaining risks written in the work standard? III&IV
questions.judgement_criteria: A review of risk records shows that the majority of risks are covered by administrative and engineering technical controls.

questions.text: Is there a regular training and communication program on site for teammates exposed to chemical hazards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Risk assessments are performed for all identified Chemical hazards according to Global Chemical Management procedure before they are approved to be used on site. There is a Hazard Communication Program (HCP) on site and required teammates are trained regularly to handle the specific chemical hazard types to which they may be exposed in their work area. Chemical air sampling protocol is in place for the site, and testing is done periodically.

questions.text: Is there a Chemical air sampling protocol in place for the site?
questions.judgement_criteria: Risk assessments are performed for all identified Chemical hazards according to Global Chemical Management procedure before they are approved to be used on site. There is a Hazard Communication Program (HCP) on site and required teammates are trained regularly to handle the specific chemical hazard types to which they may be exposed in their work area. Chemical air sampling protocol is in place for the site, and testing is done periodically.

questions.text: Has the training been conducted so that the fire extinguishing equipment can be operated appropriately?
questions.judgement_criteria: Fire extinguishing equipment trainings are properly planned and implemented.

questions.text: Do you comply with safety working standards related to Mixing MUST 11? MUST11
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates can work based on Mixing MUST 11. MUST11

questions.text: Do you review the results of periodical inspections on electrical wirings?
questions.judgement_criteria: Electric systems (wiring/equipment) status are inspected and corrective actions are taken. /

questions.text: Feedback from the trainees is included when reviewing training program?
questions.judgement_criteria: The effect of training is reviewed and feed back from trainees is taken into consideration.

questions.text: Are safety rules developed/adjusted after these evaluations take place? */
questions.judgement_criteria: teammates contribute to the development of the safety rules applicable to them by evaluating these together with management to consolidate their knowledge of risks involved in work. *

questions.text: Are Work at height controls reviewed and adapted after new risks are identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Work at height controls are reviewed and improved when new risks have been identified.

questions.text: Are Mobile Equipment controls reviewed and adapted after new risks are identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Controls related to the use of mobile equipment are reviewed and improved when new risks have been identified.

questions.text: Are the results from KY used to develop new safety rules or adjust old rules or improvement of safety measures for hazards((risk mitigation/administrative measures)? KY(/)
questions.judgement_criteria: KYT results are efforts have resulted in the development/adjustment of safety rules or improvement of measures for hazards(risk mitigation/administrative measures). KYT(/

questions.text: Is there a plan of periodical activities to increase employee's awareness on Ergonomics?
questions.judgement_criteria: Ergonomic risks assessments are performed when new tasks are included or new risks are identified. There is a plan to periodically increase employee's awareness on Ergonomics RA

questions.judgement_criteria: The site recognizes residual fire risks and conduct fire prevention measures.

questions.text: Are trainings (materials/methods) updated with industry best practices?
questions.judgement_criteria: Training curricula are kept up-to-date to industry *best-practices and latest authority guidance's. *

questions.text: Are industry best practices being considered to conduct counter measure for incidents (industrial accidents, fire accidents, near misses/hidden dangers reports and recurrence preventions)?
questions.judgement_criteria: The Near-Misses / Hidden Dangers reporting is reviewed by regular benchmarking with other sites and industry best practices.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on LOTO from other plants/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure? /LOTO

questions.judgement_criteria: The LOTO standard is kept up-to-date with industry and internal best-practices as well as the feedback from those working with the standard. LOTOLOTO

questions.text: Is feedback from the teammates considered when adapting the safety rules?
questions.judgement_criteria: The effectiveness of safety rules is improved by incorporating other plant's/industry best-practice and feedback from teammates on a periodic basis. /

questions.judgement_criteria: The Work at height standard is periodically reviewed and updated by the incorporation of internal and industry best-practices and the feedback from teammates performing Work at height.

questions.text: Is feedback from the Work at height standard users considered when adapting the Work at height standard?
questions.judgement_criteria: The Work at height standard is periodically reviewed and updated by the incorporation of internal and industry best-practices and the feedback from teammates performing Work at height.

questions.text: Have you gathered best practices on mobile equipment from other plants/industry, and considered to incorporate them into the site procedure? /
questions.judgement_criteria: The safety controls applied to the use of mobile equipment are subject to periodic review through which the feedback of teammates, internal and industry best-practices are incorporated.

questions.text: Is feedback from the mobile equipment users considered when adapting the mobile equipment standard?
questions.judgement_criteria: The safety controls applied to the use of mobile equipment are subject to periodic review through which the feedback of teammates, internal and industry best-practices are incorporated.

questions.judgement_criteria: The site is benchmarking its 3S results and systems with other sites, and looking for more solutions to improve 3S even further. 3S3S

questions.judgement_criteria: Contractor employees who perform construction/works in BS have required skills and certification(E.g. crane and hoist). //(

questions.judgement_criteria: Large Tire Manual Handling Operations are identified. A procedure exists and teammates are aware of it.

questions.text: Have Ergonomics hazards been identified on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: Ergonomics hazards have been identified on site

questions.text: Are all chemicals used on site (production and non-production chemicals) listed in a list of approved chemicals, and each has a corresponding Material Use Form (MUF) and current and compliant Safety Data Sheet (SDS)? SDS

questions.judgement_criteria: Is there a list of all approved chemicals for the site, along with a corresponding Material Use Form (MUF) and Safety Data Sheet (SDS), and these records are kept according to local regulation.Required employees are aware of Chemical hazards. SDS

questions.text: Is SBU/site local maintenance safety management procedure trained to maintenance staff? SBU/
questions.judgement_criteria: General safety management for maintenance requirements have been communicated and both maintenance managers and staffs are aware of General safety management for maintenance.

questions.text: Are you aware of fire risks in your job position/services?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates understand the fire risks.

questions.text: Have employees experienced evacuation routes and procedures during evacuation drills?
questions.judgement_criteria: The evacuation drill are conducted for all employees working in the site.

questions.text: Do you standardize the inspection points and maintenance plan for electrical wiring?
questions.judgement_criteria: Electric systems (wiring/equipment) that have to be checked are listed and updated properly. /

questions.text: Are the causes of accidents/incidents that have occurred at this site communicated to teammates and is this shown by communication records?
questions.judgement_criteria: The site has a process to communicate the cause of accidents timely as indicated by communication records.

questions.judgement_criteria: Site Safety Manager leader shares the incident or fire reports from other sites.

questions.text: Is there a list of licensed crane operators?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is a list of licensed and trained crane operators who are aware of past incidents and know how to prevent reoccurrence of these.

questions.text: Are the crane operators aware of past incidents and know the procedures to avoid these from happening again?
questions.judgement_criteria: There is a list of licensed and trained crane operators who are aware of past incidents and know how to prevent reoccurrence of these.

questions.text: Is there a risk tracking on site?
questions.judgement_criteria: Incident investigation results are used to supplement the risk tracking either by registering the causation as a new risk or adjusting control levels.

questions.text: Are new risks added to the risk tracking or safety controls adjusted after incident investigations?
questions.judgement_criteria: Incident investigation results are used to supplement the risk tracking either by registering the causation as a new risk or adjusting control levels.

questions.text: Is KYT are conducted by all employee on site? KYT
questions.judgement_criteria: KY is conducted by everyone on site. KY

questions.text: Are pre-work meetings conducted prior to the start of work, are risks identified and risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined. /

questions.text: Are there criteria for selecting contractors and are contracts awarded in accordance with those criteria? Are contracts renewed at a regular frequency?
questions.judgement_criteria: The contractor selection criteria are established based on regulatory, Bridgestone, and/or site safety requirements. And contractor is reviewed based on these criteria and judged to determine if the contractor may be selected for work.

questions.text: Is the large tire manual handling operation procedure reviewed in response to incidents?
questions.judgement_criteria: The large tire manual handling procedure is reviewed in response to incidents

questions.text: Have employees been made aware of the Ergonomics hazards of their jobs and when they are increasing their ergonomic risks due to poor techniques or not using tools provided to prevent these hazards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Employees are aware of Ergonomic hazards

questions.text: Are countermeasures in place to reduce exposure to Chemical Hazards?
questions.judgement_criteria: There are countermeasures in place to reduce exposure to Chemical Hazards (e.g., gloves, local exhaust, etc.) according to the information on Material Use Form (MUF), Safety Data Sheet (SDS), and PPE hazard assessment. MUF)SDS

questions.text: Do you hold the premeeting before maintenance work? And during this meeting do you identify the risks and determine risk mitigation & control? /
questions.judgement_criteria: Maintenance manager responding to risks and sharing information as defined in the standard before to start the maintenance work.

questions.text: Are the identified risks and determined risks mitigation & control shared to maintenance staff.
questions.judgement_criteria: Before maintenance, maintenance technicians join KY activity/tool box meeting and able to understand the shared information/cautions in work. KY

questions.text: Do the maintenance technicians recognize what actions they should take when any unexpected / uncontrolled change occurs or new risks are identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Before maintenance, maintenance technicians join KY activity/tool box meeting and able to understand the shared information/cautions in work. KY

questions.judgement_criteria: Fire prevention control on the flammable materials are trained/educated to the teammates who may treat.

questions.text: Do you prepare the proper curing to prevent spreading sparks during hot work?
questions.judgement_criteria: When conducting hot work, the curing to prevent spreading sparks are properly conducted.

questions.judgement_criteria: The fire prevention training are conducted proper frequency/method.

questions.judgement_criteria: Site safety rules and are communicated by management structurally as indicated by worker statements and communication records.

questions.text: After incidents occur, do you know what actions should be taken to prevent a recurrence?
questions.judgement_criteria: The Safety Leader is knowledgeable in the Recurrence Prevention Procedure of incidents (industrial accidents, fire accidents, near misses/hidden dangers reports and recurrence preventions) occurred and complies with recommendations and countermeasure implementation instructions.

questions.judgement_criteria: LOTO procedures are understood and being followed. LOTO

questions.judgement_criteria: Site tour(s) indicate that observed operators use mobile equipment in accordance to prescribed controls.

questions.text: Are all unnecessary objects being removed from the work area?

questions.judgement_criteria: All unnecessary objects are being removed from the workspace and necessary items have a fixed and clearly marked position which is respected by the teammates.

questions.text: Do you know crane and hoist operation rules in your process??
questions.judgement_criteria: Crane operators are aware of the safety rules applicable to them and show adherence to these as indicated during the site tour(s) and worker statements.

questions.judgement_criteria: Crane operators are aware of the safety rules applicable to them and show adherence to these as indicated during the site tour(s) and worker statements.

questions.text: Is KYT conducted periodically in accordance with SBU or/and site standard by everyone on site? KYTSBU/

questions.judgement_criteria: KYT is conducted regularly and an annual KYT evaluation is conducted to improve the system. KYTKYT

questions.text: Do all contractor employees receive contractor H&S orientation/training prior to the start of the work? /

questions.text: Do employees follow proper procedure when manually handling large tires?
questions.judgement_criteria: Large Tire Manual Handling Operation Procedure follows the Global Standard and teammates follow it accordingly.

questions.text: Are risk assessments for ergonomic hazards performed following the Global Ergonomics procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: Risk assessments are performed following the Global Ergonomics procedure for all identified Ergonomic hazards.

questions.text: Are risk assessments performed during the new chemical approval process for all identified chemical hazards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Risk assessments are performed for all identified Chemical hazards according to Global Chemical Management procedure before they are approved to be used on site. There is a Hazard Communication Program (HCP) on site and required teammates are trained regularly to handle the specific chemical hazard types to which they may be exposed in their work area. Chemical air sampling protocol is in place for the site, and testing is done periodically.

questions.text: Before maintenance work, does the maintenance manager confirms that countermeasures established working standards/pre-working meeting have been correctly implemented?
questions.judgement_criteria: Before maintenance work, the maintenance manager confirms that countermeasures established working standards and derived from KY activity are in place. KY

questions.text: Do you inspect fire risks in your own process/area/department periodically?
questions.judgement_criteria: Fire risks in the site are periodically inspected.

questions.text: Do you check the storage condition of flammable materials through patrols etc.?
questions.judgement_criteria: The flammable material’s storage status are checked and maintained.

questions.text: Do you standardize the management of hot work?
questions.judgement_criteria: The designated hot work area are specified and certified.

questions.text: Do you observe/inspect to verify that hot work procedures are complied?
questions.judgement_criteria: When conducting hot work, person in charge correctly conduct patrol.

questions.text: Do you create the procedure to check personnel safety on employees and contractors when disaster occurs? (e.g. count the number of persons at designated evacuation point when evacuation from the buildings is needed)
questions.judgement_criteria: Personnel safety confirmation on all employee safety are ensured in a prescribed methods (e.g., roll call) at evacuation drill.

questions.text: Do you analyze the causes and implement countermeasures for LOTO violations? LOTO
questions.judgement_criteria: When a LOTO safety rule violation is identified, investigate root causes, and take corrective action for the causes. LOTO

questions.text: Are monitoring/patrol methods for LOTO activity reviewed and considered for consistency with monitoring/patrol results, occurrence injury, and multiple monitoring instruments (if do), and updated as necessary? LOTO
questions.judgement_criteria: Compliance monitoring/audits are performed on LOTO activity at a defined timing and frequency. LOTO

questions.text: Do teammates engage management about and evaluate the risks involved in their work?
questions.judgement_criteria: teammates contribute to the development of the safety rules applicable to them by evaluating these together with management to consolidate their knowledge of risks involved in work. *

questions.text: Do teammates proceed with 3S activity for the improvement of 3S level? 3S3S()
questions.judgement_criteria: 3S is carried out autonomously by everyone on shift for improvement of 3S level and is considered a base expectation. 3S3S

questions.text: Are the progress of 3S activity reported for top management and reviewed? 3S
questions.judgement_criteria: Conducted 3S activities are evaluated by monitoring and the results are reviewed by top management. 3S

questions.text: Do crane operators provide feedback(safety perspective) to their peers inside and outside of their plants? SBU
questions.judgement_criteria: Crane operators occasionally go and observe crane operations in the plant or in other plants and provide feedback to their peers.

questions.text: Do you perform risk assessment to tasks?
questions.judgement_criteria: The risk tracking shows that RA and risk mitigation have been performed to not only equipment, but also tasks. RA

questions.text: Countermeasures for risk are assessed and are it reviewed as necessary?
questions.judgement_criteria: Counter measures based on RA are validated and if need, are considered revised/updated. RA

questions.text: Does BS perform safety patrols of contractor's work area, and confirm that risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined at the pre-work meeting are conducted? BS
questions.judgement_criteria: BS perform safety patrols of contractor's work area, and confirm that risk mitigation measures/administrative controls defined at the pre-work meeting are conducted. BS

questions.text: Is the procedure reviewed when new activities are introduced on the site or when teammates offer relevant feedback?
questions.judgement_criteria: Large tire manual operations are reviewed whenever a new task or activity is introduced. Teammates receive periodical training refreshments in the procedure when required (e.g. returning from absence, changes in procedure)

questions.text: Is there evidence of large tire manual handling procedure training refreshments being performed for the relevant teammates when required?
questions.judgement_criteria: Large tire manual operations are reviewed whenever a new task or activity is introduced. Teammates receive periodical training refreshments in the procedure when required (e.g. returning from absence, changes in procedure)

questions.text: Are ergonomic risk assessment triggered when new tasks, and/or new or modified equipment are installed, in order for new risks to be identified? RA
questions.judgement_criteria: Ergonomic risks assessments are performed when new tasks are included or new risks are identified. There is a plan to periodically increase employee's awareness on Ergonomics RA

questions.text: Is there any risk mitigation control taken above legal requirements and/or local regulations?
questions.judgement_criteria: Risk mitigation controls above legal requirements and/or local regulations are implemented for chemicals. (e.g. Regarding chemical which requires no engineering controls, they have been added for risk mitigation as site rules.

questions.text: Do you promote the improvements on safety working standards and/or working environment based on RA outcome or safety proposals from teammate? RA
questions.judgement_criteria: Management review/draft the plan based on that was reported by the results of risk assessment and feedback from maintenance operator. /

questions.text: Has an MOC Coordinator, an MOC reviewer and final approver been defined with clear roles an responsibilities defined?
questions.judgement_criteria: Changes are managed by an appointed MOC coordinator, a reviewer and change approver who evaluate the legal compliance of a proposed change structurally.

questions.text: Are proposed changes evaluated in a structured manner that considers the legal compliance?
questions.judgement_criteria: Changes are managed by an appointed MOC coordinator, a reviewer and change approver who evaluate the legal compliance of a proposed change structurally.

questions.text: Are trainings needs and content reviewed and edited after an accident occurs?
questions.judgement_criteria: Updating training material or providing additional training is reviewed when accidents occur.

questions.text: Did you take measures to recently incidents*? *Refer to “Safety Maturity Assessment Guidelines” (Manufacturing) Appendix 4 *4
questions.judgement_criteria: Countermeasures following of incidents (industrial accidents, fire accidents, near misses/hidden dangers reports and recurrence preventions) are implemented.

questions.text: Have change initiators been adequately trained to fulfil their duties?
questions.judgement_criteria: Change initiators and approvers have been trained to fulfil their duties based on a local process in preparation to change on safety, IH and DP.

questions.text: Is the effectiveness of implemented countermeasures reviewed with the reporting individual, H&S and maintenance department?
questions.judgement_criteria: Reports of incidents (industrial accidents, fire accidents, and near misses, hidden dangers reports) are stored according to local legislations. The effectiveness of the countermeasures is evaluated.

questions.text: Are the site's safety rules analyzed in depth and are the risks regarding the change evaluated considered when a proposed change is being deliberated? 3M/4M
questions.judgement_criteria: Site safety rules and work standards are analyzed to identify how the proposed change might affect these positively or negatively. /
Site safety rules and work standards are analyzed to identify how the proposed change might affect these positively or negatively. /

questions.text: Does the material of contractor H&S orientation/training includes emergency procedures, introduction to the Safety Mission Statement and Safety fundamental Activities? /
questions.judgement_criteria: All contractors receive H&S orientation/training prior to the work. The material includes emergency procedures, introduction to the Safety Mission Statement and Safety fundamental Activities. //

questions.text: Are JSA / JHA / KY conducted before emergency maintenance tasks? JSA*JHAKY *Job Safety Analysis
questions.judgement_criteria: Before emergency maintenance, other hazard identification method such as JSA(Job Safety Analysis), JHA(Job Hazard Analysis), KY or similar shall take place. And these information are shared and record. /JSA(Job Safety Analysis), JHA(Job Hazard Analysis), KY

questions.text: Do you conduct GENBA observation on maintenance tasks? What do you do when insufficient safety measures or violation of rules are found?
questions.judgement_criteria: Maintenance manager and staff could detect that unexpected or uncontrolled change or discovered new risk occurs during maintenance works. This response procedure has been defined and documented.

questions.text: Do teammates communicate hazards and risks to their supervisors as soon as they identify them?
questions.judgement_criteria: Management informs teammates as soon as reasonably possible about newly found hazards and risks. Teammates communicate hazards and risks immediately to their supervisor.

questions.text: Are risk assessments reviewed for similar task/service/equipment when incidents (industrial accidents, fire accidents, near misses/hidden dangers reports and recurrence preventions) were reported? RA
questions.judgement_criteria: A recognition program to promote Near-Misses / Hidden Danger reporting is in place. Risk Assessments are reviewed during the investigation of a near-miss where applicable.

questions.text: Do risk assessments addressing the new possible risks take place before a change is implemented? RA
questions.judgement_criteria: The potential hazards and risks involved in a change are subject to risk assessment in preparation to that change. Controls are implemented before the change is made.

questions.text: Are preventative controls implemented before the change is made?
questions.judgement_criteria: The potential hazards and risks involved in a change are subject to risk assessment in preparation to that change. Controls are implemented before the change is made.

questions.text: Do you take corrective/improvement actions for flammable material storage areas based on fire prevention inspection or patrol feedbacks?
questions.judgement_criteria: The flammable materials storage methods are planned/conducted improvements based on inspection person in charge feedbacks.

questions.text: Do you respond appropriately for identified failures or violations?
questions.judgement_criteria: Corrective actions are properly taken when any failures on hot work rules are identified.

questions.text: Are any corrective actions taken when opportunity for improvements are identified through fire fighting training/drill?
questions.judgement_criteria: Based on the inspections results, the methods of fire extinguishing equipment, standards, and training methods have been reviewed.

questions.text: When a defect is found during the MUST11 inspection, do you improve the defect at that time? MUST11
questions.judgement_criteria: Results of Mixing MUST11 inspection are reviewed by site top management. MUST11

questions.text: Are corrective actions on emergency preparedness procedures taken when any opportunity for improvements are identified through training/drill?
questions.judgement_criteria: Evacuation (training) method is reviewed/revised based on failures that have been detected during evacuation drills.

questions.text: Do you review the prevent measures on electrical wirings based on periodical inspections/maintenance?
questions.judgement_criteria: Electric system(wiring/equipment) inspection points/items are reviewed based on feedbacks from in charge of inspectors. /

questions.text: Are changes designed, planned, executed and evaluated based on pre-defined safety criteria which include hazards, risks and industry best-practices? PDCA
questions.judgement_criteria: Changes are designed, planned, executed and evaluated based on pre-defined safety criteria which include hazards, risks and industry *best-practices *
